steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The blank comes between two independent clauses. The word however is an adverb, so it needs punctuation with the first clause and a semicolon before the second.

steps.title: Place however correctly
steps.content: Choice A puts a comma before however and a semicolon after it. Choice B attaches the contrast to the wrong clause. Choice C creates a comma splice, and Choice D fuses the clauses. The answer is A.
